chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-394 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Fresh Case 1.For orders on CMA 1839/25 2.For orders on CMA 1840/25 3.For orders on CMA 1841/25 4.For hearing of main case 25-03-2025 Mr. Muhammad Yousuf Mahar, Advocate for petitioners. **** 1. Urgent application is granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 3&4. It is, inter alia, contended by counsel for the petitioners that the petitioners are employees of Pakistan Post Office and they were appointed after observing all due process of law and are rendering their services. He further contends that the Prime Minister constituted an Inquiry Commission to investigate the recruitments made in the department during the past 10 years reviewing both the hiring need and process and acting upon the same, the Inquiry Commission has recommended that newly recruited candidates may be made to undergo specially designed tests to assess the adequacy of their skills and competence and that the candidates whose performance reflects significant variance from the results and marks obtained during the recruitment process may be processed against subject to due process of law. The Ministry of Communication must ensure the quality, transparency and integrity of this process. Thereafter the test and interview process qua recruitment was initiated. He further submits 2 that once the recruitment was duly made, the same cannot be subjected to another recruitment process and that in case, the petitioners have committed any offence, they may only be processed under the law.

Thereafter the test and interview process qua recruitment was initiated. He further submits 2 that once the recruitment was duly made, the same cannot be subjected to another recruitment process and that in case, the petitioners have committed any offence, they may only be processed under the law. Hence, the process adopted at the instance of Prime Minister is politically motivated in order to get rid of the petitioners and made a room for fresh political appointments. Contentions raised require consideration. Let notices be issued to respondents and DAG for 10.04.2025 to be taken up along with identical matters. Meanwhile, process of test and interview in respect of the petitioners shall remain suspended till further orders. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad 3 Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-392 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Fresh Case 1.For orders on CMA 1832/25 2.For orders on office objections 3.For orders on CMA 1833/25 4.For orders on CMA 1834/25 5.For hearing of main case 25-03-2025 Mr. Shahid Ali Memon, Advocate for petitioner. **** 1. Urgent application is granted. 2. Office objection(s) are to be complied with within one week. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4&5. It is, inter alia, contended by counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner is employee of Pakistan Post Office and he was appointed after observing all due process of law and is discharging his duties. He further contends that the Prime Minister constituted an Inquiry Commission to investigate the recruitments made in the department during the past 10 years reviewing both the hiring need and process and acting upon the same, the Inquiry Commission has recommended that newly recruited candidates may be made to undergo specially designed tests to assess the adequacy of their skills and competence and that the candidates whose performance reflects significant variance from the results and marks obtained during the recruitment process may be processed against subject to due process of law.

It is, inter alia, contended by counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner is employee of Pakistan Post Office and he was appointed after observing all due process of law and is discharging his duties. He further contends that the Prime Minister constituted an Inquiry Commission to investigate the recruitments made in the department during the past 10 years reviewing both the hiring need and process and acting upon the same, the Inquiry Commission has recommended that newly recruited candidates may be made to undergo specially designed tests to assess the adequacy of their skills and competence and that the candidates whose performance reflects significant variance from the results and marks obtained during the recruitment process may be processed against subject to due process of law. The Ministry of Communication must ensure the quality, transparency and 4 integrity of this process. Thereafter the test and interview process qua recruitment was initiated. He further submits that once the recruitment was duly made, the same cannot be subjected to another recruitment process and that in case, the petitioner has committed any offence, he may only be processed under the law. Hence, the process adopted at the instance of Prime Minister is politically motivated in order to get rid of the petitioner and made a room for fresh political appointments. Contentions raised require consideration. Let notices be issued to respondents and DAG for 10.04.2025 to be taken up along with identical matters. Meanwhile, process of test and interview in respect of the petitioner shall remain suspended till further orders. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
